title: Jatropha flabellifolia
taxonomy: Jatropha flabellifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It belongs to the genus Jatropha, which is a group of plants native to the Americas and Africa. The family Euphorbiaceae, also known as the spurge family, includes over 2,800 species of plants, many of which are known for their medicinal properties. The genus Jatropha is further divided into several subgenera and sections, with Jatropha flabellifolia being a member of one of these sections. The classification of Jatropha flabellifolia reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Euphorbiaceae family.

side_effects: Jatropha flabellifolia, also known as the fanleaf jatropha, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, but its use may come with potential side effects. The plant contains toxic compounds, such as phorbol esters, which can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting the plant's seeds or leaves can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Long-term consumption may cause more severe health issues, including liver damage and kidney problems. Further research is needed to fully understand the side effects and potential risks associated with using Jatropha flabellifolia.
intro: Jatropha flabellifolia, also known as Mexican Jatropha or Devil's Tree, is a plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family, characterized by its unique, fan-shaped leaves and bright yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Jatropha flabellifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make various herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including skin conditions, fever, and respiratory issues.
